skill.name: azure-kusto
skill.description: Query Azure Data Explorer databases with KQL, inspect schemas, list Kusto resources, and analyze logs, telemetry, and time series data.
skill.summary: Analyze Azure Data Explorer data with guided KQL queries and Kusto resource workflows.
skill.author: microsoft

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many Ruby backtick and weak cryptography patterns, but these are Markdown KQL examples and inline command names. The meaningful risk is the documented Azure CLI fallback, which can make authenticated network calls to Azure Data Explorer. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Azure CLI Fallback Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill advises using Azure CLI and az rest when MCP tools fail. This is legitimate for Azure Data Explorer work. It can query cloud data using the user's Azure identity.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command guidance is explicit and reachable during fallback. It is not malicious, but it can perform authenticated network operations against Azure resources.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance findings are Markdown KQL snippets, inline KQL function names, headings, or descriptive text. No executable Ruby code, cryptographic operation, or host reconnaissance instruction was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are contained in Markdown code fences, tables, or prose. The file has no script body or executable Ruby context.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Call to Azure Data Explorer Endpoint
dangerous_patterns.description: The fallback command posts to a Kusto REST query endpoint. This matches the skill purpose, but users should confirm the target cluster, database, and query before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint is documented as a placeholder for ADX REST queries. The risk depends on user-provided Azure context and permissions.

content.value_statement: Kusto and ADX workflows often require precise KQL, schema context, and safe query limits. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through KQL analysis, schema discovery, and Azure Data Explorer troubleshooting.
actual_capabilities: Builds KQL queries for Azure Data Explorer tables and databases.
Guides schema discovery using Kusto table and database workflows.
Supports log, telemetry, IoT, security, and performance analysis scenarios.
Explains aggregation, joins, time bucketing, and chart rendering patterns.
Provides Azure CLI fallback guidance when MCP Kusto tools are unavailable.
limitations: Requires valid Azure access and Kusto database permissions.
Does not replace database access controls or query approval processes.
Does not support SQL, NoSQL, Elasticsearch, or non-Azure analytics platforms.
Long or broad queries can still time out or increase cloud resource usage.

use_cases.title: Investigate Production Incidents
use_cases.description: Create focused KQL queries that filter recent events, correlate logs, and summarize error patterns.

use_cases.title: Explore Telemetry Datasets
use_cases.description: Inspect schemas, select useful columns, and create time series summaries for device or application telemetry.

use_cases.title: Review Security Events
use_cases.description: Analyze SIEM-style event data with joins, severity filters, and grouped counts across time windows.

prompt_templates.title: List Available Kusto Resources
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me list my Azure Data Explorer clusters and databases for this subscription. Ask for any missing Azure context first.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect a Table Schema
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me inspect the schema for a Kusto table, then suggest useful columns for filtering, grouping, and time analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Incident Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a KQL query for recent errors in my application logs. Include a time filter, grouping by error type, and a small result limit.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize a Complex Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my KQL analysis plan for performance. Recommend filters, projections, joins, aggregations, and safe limits before execution.

output_examples.input: Find the most common application errors from the last hour.
output_examples.output: A focused query plan with a one-hour time filter, error severity filtering, grouped counts by error type, and a limited results table.

output_examples.input: Show response time trends for the last day.
output_examples.output: A time series analysis plan using five-minute buckets, average latency, median latency, and high-percentile latency metrics.

output_examples.input: I need to understand a new telemetry table.
output_examples.output: A schema discovery workflow, suggested key columns, recommended filters, and next-step questions about time fields and identifiers.

best_practices: Confirm subscription, cluster, database, and table names before running queries.
Use time filters, projected columns, and result limits for exploratory analysis.
Review generated queries before using Azure CLI fallback commands.
anti_patterns: Running broad queries without a time range or row limit.
Using this skill for SQL, NoSQL, or non-Azure analytics services.
Sending sensitive query results to external systems without approval.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It helps create and run Azure Data Explorer workflows using KQL, schema discovery, and Kusto resource operations.

faq.question: Does it require Azure credentials?
faq.answer: Yes. Query execution and resource listing require valid Azure authentication and permissions for the target Kusto resources.

faq.question: Can it write or delete data?
faq.answer: The documented workflows focus on querying, listing resources, and schema inspection. Review any generated command before execution.

faq.question: Can it optimize slow KQL queries?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest time filters, projections, limits, aggregations, and join improvements for better query performance.

faq.question: Does it support SQL databases?
faq.answer: No. It is focused on Azure Data Explorer and KQL, not relational SQL databases or NoSQL stores.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill includes Azure CLI fallback commands that can make authenticated network requests to ADX endpoints.
